title: ossaea retropila
intro: Ossaea retropila, also known as the Panama rose or night-blooming jasmine, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Pentaphylacaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ossaea retropila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and provide relief from fever and cough. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its flowers can also be used in traditional medicine, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
